name: Marugame Udon
tagline: Locals genuinely love it despite the Waikiki address — watch them pull fresh noodles through the glass.
mustOrder: kamaage udon ($5–7, the purist's choice — plain dipping noodles, taste the quality)
parking: Don't drive — no parking in Waikiki
talkStory: Part of Japan's Toridoll Holdings chain (800+ locations worldwide), the Waikiki outpost opened in 2011 and immediately drew lines around the block — it's been that way for over a decade. Modeled after Kagawa Prefecture's self-serve sanuki udon shops where you watch noodles being hand-pulled through glass. The cafeteria format (grab tray, choose udon, pick tempura, pay) keeps bills under $10, making it Waikiki's best food value. Barack Obama was spotted here during holiday visits.
